answers: Ventriloquism is the art of speaking without moving your lips, creating the illusion that a puppet or dummy is talking. It combines voice manipulation, comedy, and puppet performance into an entertaining act.

Ventriloquists substitute lip movements with tongue and throat techniques for difficult letters like B, F, M, P, V, and W. With practice, the audience can't tell the performer's lips are barely moving. Misdirection helps too!

Famous ventriloquists include Edgar Bergen, Jeff Dunham (the highest-grossing comedian in North America), Terry Fator (AGT winner), and Nina Conti. Jeff Dunham's characters like Achmed and Walter are iconic.

Learning ventriloquism takes dedication — start by practicing speaking without lip movement in front of a mirror. Master substitute sounds for tricky letters. A good puppet and strong comedic writing complete the act.
